name: X-Force: Successful Outbound Connection to a Remote Proxy or Anonymization Service
notes:
This rule triggers when a communication with a remote proxy or an anonymization service has been observed. These services typically hide the originating address of the Source IP.

Note: The Confidence Factor ranges between 0 to 100. On assets of lower importance, an administrator might weigh an X-Force rule to trigger on a higher confidence factor of 75. When tuning rules, customers should think of a scale where 50 is the tipping point.

See the blog https://ibm.biz/BdPEXv for details.
conditions_text: when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
when the flow context is Local to Remote
when the destination port is one of the following 25
when Destination IP is categorized by X-Force as Anonymization Services with confidence value greater than 75
TD_list: APPLY X-Force: Successful Outbound Connection to a Remote Proxy or Anonymization Service on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
AND when the flow context is Local to Remote
AND NOT when the destination port is one of the following 25
AND when Destination IP is categorized by X-Force as Anonymization Services with confidence value greater than 75
RED_list: Event name: Connection to a remote proxy or anonymization service (Outbound)
Event description: These services typically hide the originating address of the Source IP.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
